title: Aliphatic Amines Group - Canada.ca
content:
## Aliphatic Amines Group
These substances were identified for action under the Chemicals Management Plan (CMP). The screening assessment focuses on 13 substances referred to collectively under the CMP as the Aliphatic Amines Group. The ecological risk of the 9 long-chain aliphatic amines identified in the table below was assessed using a class-based approach that encompasses a wider set of both monoamine and diamine substances, as detailed in the screening assessment. It includes but is not limited to the 9 long-chain aliphatic amines identified in the table below.

Anticipated publication of the Final Screening Assessment for the Aliphatic Amines Group and the Risk Management Approach for Long-chain Aliphatic Amines, including DPDAB .
Publication in Canada Gazette , Part I, of a notice under section 71 of CEPA 1999.
Publication and start of 60-day public comment period on the Draft Screening Assessment for the Aliphatic Amines Group and the Risk Management Scope for Long-chain Aliphatic Amines, including DPDAB . The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 155, No. 10 .

## Information gathering
In June 2023, a notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 157, No. 25 – June 24, 2023 under section 71 of CEPA 1999. This notice applies to 84 aliphatic amine substances. More information is available on the Information gathering with respect to certain substances under the Chemicals Management Plan web page .
Six other substances were identified as being a part of the Aliphatic Amines Group at the outset of the third phase of the CMP . Four of these 6 substances were subsequently determined to be of low concern to both human health and the environment, through other approaches. The conclusion for the substance with CAS RN 68955-53-3 is provided in the Final Screening Assessment for Substances Identified as Being of Low Concern based on the Ecological Risk Classification of Organic Substances and the Threshold of Toxicological Concern (TTC)-based Approach for Certain Substances . The conclusions for substances with CAS RNs 112-90-3, 80939-62-4 and 90367-27-4 are provided in the Final Screening Assessment for the Rapid Screening of Substances with Limited General Population Exposure . The remaining 2 substances (CAS RNs 108-91-8 and 58713-21-6) were moved from the Aliphatic Amines Group into the Sodium Cyclamate and Cyclohexylamine Group and the Hexamethylenetriamine Group , respectively.
